name: LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations)

performance.notes: Generates perturbed samples (default 5000) — slow for complex models; no GPU support

agent_readiness.notes: Local model-agnostic explanations via locally-fitted linear approximations. from lime.lime_tabular import LimeTabularExplainer. explainer = LimeTabularExplainer(X_train, feature_names=names, class_names=['0','1'], mode='classification'). exp = explainer.explain_instance(X_test[0], model.predict_proba, num_features=10). exp.show_in_notebook(). exp.as_list() for feature importance. Text: LimeTextExplainer. Image: LimeImageExplainer. Works with any sklearn-compatible predict_proba. Slower and less stable than SHAP but supports text and image explanations. Development has slowed vs SHAP.

gotchas: Explanations are locally consistent but not globally stable
Slow for large num_samples (5000 model calls per explanation)
Development activity has declined vs SHAP
best_when: When SHAP doesn't support the model type
Quick local explanations without SHAP value computation
avoid_when: Tabular data explanations — SHAP is more accurate and faster
Global feature importance — SHAP global plots are better
High-frequency explanations (too slow for production serving)
summary: LIME generates local model-agnostic explanations by fitting linear approximations around individual predictions. LimeTabularExplainer(X_train).explain_instance(X_test[0], model.predict_proba). LimeTextExplainer for text classifiers. LimeImageExplainer for CNNs. Works with any black-box model. Non-deterministic (set random_state). Development has slowed vs SHAP. Best for text/image explanations. For tabular data use SHAP — more accurate and globally consistent.
